reviews.review_text:
Replaying this game after a really long time, and now that i'm older, i understand the lore much better and its themes. Omori has an amazing lore (you'll probably cry in the end), good art direction and really fun gameplay if you enjoy rpg maker games, the ost is also INCREDIBLE, and combined with the lore, it's what makes this game so good.

Also i love basil.
OMORI is basically what happens when years of unresolved guilt, repression, and avoidance finally catch up to you. 10/10 experience, still not emotionally recovered.

This game has to be my favourite game of all time, i remember seeing it from a youtube video describing why many people origionally thought it was a scam, and im so thankful i got it.
This is the only game other than "Until then" That has been able to live in the back of my head rent free wondering what would happen after the multiple endings.
I suggest you go through this game at your own pace and stop to enjoy the game in its entirety and think about what some deeping meanings within some of the dialouge may be, and who knows? Maybe you become like me who bought both of the kel plushies and is also trying to get all acheivements.

When the moon hits your eye, Like a big pizza pie, that's Omori...

the game is wonderful, it has a beautiful yet disturbing story, cool and cute characters, and the music is just as good

there are some negative points that i will mention now

the game's text is in some parts, very unnecessary, it's too long and sometimes adds nothing to the story

the battle and upgrade system in the game is exciting only at the beginning, after you reach level 30 it becomes so easy to level up that you could beat the game in 15 minutes if it weren't for the slow text and cutscenes

the gameplay gets repetitive towards the end of the game, aside from the fact that some things in the game can't be redone, like the chicken on top of the pyramid (just my opinion)

i spent 100 hours conquering everything in the game, it was good but I felt like there was more work to be done in the game, some places are empty and there's nothing to do, some don't even have npcs

i love this game, but it has some flaws that i hate in a modern rpg

i give it a score of 9.2

Peak, got really attached to the characters, I very much reccomend it, the story also was amazing.
had to 100% it.

Just amazing, 10/10.

Amazing story about how bottled up trauma and depression can affect your life and the lives of others, And a wonderful message about seeking support from people who love and care about you, while also having the courage to face and overcome your fears. The struggle with trauma and mental health being presented as a horror experience also made it hit harder in all the ways that it needed to. Genuine gem of a game.

Quite possibly my favorite game ever. It meant so much to me when I first played it during a low point in my life, to every subsequent playthrough afterwards. It is just so special, and there isn't much more to say than that.

Omori is an RPG about a teenage boy named Sunny and his dream-world alter ego, Omori. Gameplay is split into two settings: Sunny's activities in Faraway Town and Omori's exploration of a dream world. How Sunny and Omori interact depends on the player’s choices, resulting in one of several endings, in which Sunny either overcomes or suppresses his fears and repressed memories. I remember coming across it from Nitro Rad, who talked extensively about the game that piqued my curiosity. So with something like Omori, I was aware that it’s relatively messed up, but to what extent was something I needed to find out. I finally got to play it in preparation for a panel for an anime convention, but the panel itself was really disappointing. I’m glad it gave me a reason to play Omori, as it’s honestly up there with Undertale as one of my favorite games.

I find the graphics simultaneously charming and retro in an old-fashioned look, but fresh and new. Especially when it comes to Omori’s world, which is essentially a manifestation of how Sunny sees the world through his own experience while he sleeps, but still has a hard time grasping reality and fantasy in some elements when waking up. The characters in this game are super interesting and well-developed. Sunny, Aubrey, Kel, Hero, Basil, Mari, and Omori are all amazing characters with so much life through fantastic writing. I could make a whole video about how engaging any of these characters is in their own way, especially Sunny and Omori’s connection. Most of the game is through Omori’s perspective, and the creativity of the world-building is just very appreciative, especially in retrospect of Sunny’s connection. The combat system is simple, using emotions to give an advantage against enemies, but it’s very effective that for 98% of it, I wasn’t all that bored or felt it was drawn out. The soundtrack is also very memorable, with so many catchy tunes that can either get you bopping your head or just get mentally distraught. I haven’t been this engaged in a game for a very long time, and it made me want to keep playing just to see where it was going because I was really trying to figure this out.

When the game does get dark, it REALLY gets dark, which may get too dark for some, but it’s just right for me to really experience the dark nature of it when portraying anxiety, depression, guilt, trauma, loneliness, and the psychological mindset one has when going through life. Most of the time, it’s just cute and funny to see these colorful characters in Omori’s life, although in Sunny’s life, it’s more character-driven and grounded, seeing them at an older age and separate lives as time progresses. One of my viewers said she’s glad I’m not playing this game alone, and I didn’t get what she meant at first because, for most of the game, while sad, it doesn’t affect me too much. When the final act came in, I understood what she meant, which really started to affect me on an emotional level, something similar to Woman in the Yard that I connected with. It’s shocking, it’s stressful, and it’s emotionally devastating, which really puts into perspective of literally everything that happened in this game. It tackles things that I’ve never seen in a video game to that level, and it has a fantastic balance of the dark elements that doesn’t play it safe, but it doesn’t go too far where it feels more like shock value. I’m surprised by how underrated Omori is, which needs to be talked about more, and is a complete masterpiece that I will definitely try to go for other endings just to see what Omocat has come up with.

10/10

This game is maybe the best game I have played in my life. From the glorious Ost to the art style to the gameplay it is positively oozing with charm. Paired with a story that legitimately made me cry this game is a journey I can recommend to anybody. Loved every second of it. Other than Humphrey, I hate Humphrey.

"No bandage can stifle an eternal wound... and there will be a time when its influence will bleed through."

This game is the greatest game i have ever played in my life. I genuinely started crying, when i got to the final duet scene in good ending It mitght've been the first time i've ever cried while playing a game.
super depressing and sad story so far but i really love the combat and how the horror is controlled and not around every other corner like other games are
I really like this games art , the story is very impactful and the characters are interesting , its a good game.

To start off, I believe Omori is not just a game, but an experience in itself. 
It's story, character writing, music, art (and humour) is top off the line and will always stay in my head. The connection you build with the characters is so fleshed out and the resulting plots are incredible. The emotion system was also an interesting spin on the traditional turn based RPG games that I enjoyed and found more interesting. Everything in the game feels very though-through in its position or place on the game's timeline, and uncovering the whole story and its plots feels really great. There are few games that make me emotional, but this is one of them. Also, Basil is the best character (in my opinion) :). Truely one of my favourite games of all time.
The only thing that annoyed me a bit was the screen scaling, as the pixels don't line up correctly on a QHD monitor, so I had to play it on my second monitor so i wouldn't get differently sized pixels, so there could be some better screen scaling options.
10/10

I love the pacing, different routes, characters, and accurate portrayals of many sensitive subjects. It contains complex ideas of mental health in the form of a cute (But creepy) pixel RPG game. Since it is about mental health and sensitive topics, some things can be a bit much for some people. If you buy it, please look into some of the content warnings and such. This product is very well priced, as it can take a while to complete, can teach you many things, and is also very dopamine-inducing. 

:3

In short, I love it :3333

I think if someone truly wanted to be your friend they'll stay.

i recommend this game, it teaches a lot about mental health, i feel like this can give you an eye opening about depression especially. i hope to anyone reading this has an awesome SUNNY day :p
made me cry, but this is the best game ive ever seen. this game potrays grief so well that i started crying. this game is so frickin beautiful YALL PLAY ITTTTTT

I HIGHLY RECOMMEND GOING IN BLIND!!!

Spoilers ahead:

I feel like I'll never understand everything about this game because of how complex and deep it is. But nonetheless I adore the way the creators used colors to tell the haunting story of OMORI and the main character's use of escapism as a means to deal with his guilt. The whole game to me was highly reminiscent of pokemon for some reason which to me only amplified how this imaginary world we are stuck in half the time is like a kid's playground. The plot twist definitely caught me off guard. I was weepy towards the end. It was amazing.

i really like this game and its story because I played it a long time ago, very well made and emotional. however i had it on another system, currently i'm using a mac product and it wouldn't launch despite saying its running. otherwise i would recommend.
i LOVE omori sm, best story-based game out there. i saved up money to buy it, and i have no regrets.

I cannot explain how much this game means to me, I think about it almost everyday. The gameplay is really fun but can get redundant, especially if you are trying for 100% completion. However theres so many hidden gems and details that youre always discovering new things. The map is incredibly detailed and fun to explore and dont even get me started on the story. The story and characters are perfection and I love the soundtrack so much. I cant recommend this game enough!!

Absolutely beautiful game with a variety of endings and areas with amazing contrasts every aspect of this game was made to perfection with the deep lore and story to the gameplay and design absolutly wonderful game highly recommend to all people, the failed attempts don't feel like a loss they feel like an improvement slowly creating combos and focusing on the aspects of it all, the music and all perfectly matches the theming of the situation creating emotional depth to the game 

W Kel
Phenomenal. This game is an experience. There is no game I could recommend more.

PLAY THIS GAME PLEASE EVERYONE. LOVE THE CHARACTERS (UHUM ESPECIALLY HERO), THE STORY, THE PLOT, THE ART STYLEEEE, THE FIGHTING MECHANICS AND THE BEST PART WHICH ARE THE MUSICCCCC PHEW (no kidding they are THAT good)

I love how the game gives you a false sense of comfort, and you will be filled with unsettling feelings throughout the game. It's wholesome, very cute but also disturbing!!! (in a good way). One of the best psychological horror for sure!!

p/s you may feel a little depressed after playing it but it's a part of the experience :D
MY FAVORITE GAME EVER!! i literally love omori so much omg youre gonna regret not playing it bc its genuinely so peak

OMORI holds such a special place in my heart. I always open it up whenever I'm having a bad day, it brings me so much comfort. I wholeheartedly recommend it, even better if you don't read its description and just play blind. I did and I don't regret it in the slightest. Also Snaley is the best character.

This game is one of the best indie games i've ever played. It's got great combat, an incredible style, beautiful soundtrack but most of all the story is just so wondeful. It's message that it illustrates is what makes this medium of story telling so special

I would recommend not looking up anything related to Omocat and this game's development. It was a rocky road.


But aside from that, thank you to the developers that helped make Omori. This was truly an introspective experience that gave me a new perspective to grief. If you've lost anyone in your life that you felt like you depended on, I feel like this game will help you as well. Gameplay was reminiscent of older RPGs I liked and the world in 'both' senses was pretty good.

ALSO. If you are going for the 100%, please please PLEASE use all of the save files available to you. It will save your sanity. And make backups.

It's a great game. All the characters are really fun.
The ending really got me. I was emotional for the next few hours

Obviously there are spoilers here, I cannot stress enough how impressive and special a blind playthrough of OMORI is so try not to read too many reviews!

I love this game a lot. Every single character has depth and makes sense to be who they are within the context of the world. The game is rather particular about what sides of people are and aren't shown to give each a unique place within the story. Yes the game is easy, but I think that leaves more room for immersion, the flow of the story is not impeded at all for most people who have played an rpg before. 

The game is nearly flawless, the few flaws it does have are easily explained as choices that fit the format the game is trying to present. 

Regret is a central theme in the story, every character with dialogue over the mental age 6 has a regret as an important quality. It sets the mood of the world very well.

If you like depressed teens you should definitely play this

Its been over 2 years now when i start to play the game.
I would recommend it to EVERYONE.
The game caputre the trauma so well but in general the story and characters are written so well and they didnt hold themselves back to included sensitive topics (such as depression, sh, suicide, etc) ITS PERFECT and the ost is such a masterpiece, so well done with the instruments for each area, for each different fights and moments.
I thought at first the game was a calm and relaxing, about finding their lost friend but no i was wrong.
The way the combinded the real and dream world was such a smart move and that the story went darker and darker, even having many different endings.
The game brought me in a different pov as someone who is struggling as well since childhood, its comfortable, even still play to game (or downloads mods such as omori the dreamer, i recommend it!) and also listen ost on a daily base that im in the top 1 % listener on spotifiy second year in a row.

Please give a try! 
It may help you and someone else as it helped me.

Thank you Omocat and everyone else for creating this game!
I would buy it again and again.

Great stories and characters, one of the few games that actually made me cry. Amazing soundtracks that I can't stop listening to. The art and vibes paired with the soundtracks gave a sense of discomfort, yet so nostalgic, making such good 2d environments all around. Omori is a heavy slow-burn; sometimes, playing through some parts of the game is a slog. The gameplay loop is fine, but it is not really fun enough to keep me engaged through the slow sections of the game imo.

I played this game 2 years ago and still think about it all the time. I even got my friend to play it and watched their entire play through to relive the experience through their emotions. Incredible writing, soundtrack, and cast of characters. 

This game throws your heart into a blender. It will scare you, frustrate you, break your heart, and leave you hopelessly curious. Every moment feels like a battle between stopping to talk to everyone and inspect everything, or rushing forward as if you already have your hand on the next page of a book you can't put down, desperate to uncover more of its mysteries and stories.

DO NOT RUSH THIS STORY. Maybe light a candle. Any be ready to savor every second.

Mostly, I am not really into these type of games but many have praised it for years now. The mechanics when fighting were incredably fun, the characters felt so alive and the visuals were amazing. The undertone throughout this experience has left a mark on me. I might be late to the party, but I finally finished the story. 

Would let it break my heart again. 10/10
One of the most gripping and emotional stories i've ever witnessed. This is not a game, it's an experience.

this game changed lives , basically unforgettable if you really get into it. if you didn't cry , its whether you just don't get it or you're emotionally strong as hell. 10/10 , def recommend to try at least once.

This game is so beautiful in its rich story, I originally played and finished this game on my Xbox back in 2022 and my feelings towards this game have remained the same; the mixture of its battle sequences along with horror is done so well. To top it off its story with such a bittersweet end is done perfectly and keeps you wanting to see these characters more, to say this game is perfect is an understatement.
A genuine storytelling masterpiece with one of the most beautiful endings in all of gaming

I have completely finished Omori and wow, this game really impacted me. The ending I got was so beautiful I was holding back tears of gratefulness, for being able to experience something amazing.

Omori is the type of game that despite the aspects of horror, you still feel joy playing it.

After this, I don't really know what I'm going to do without Omori. But for now, I will start to CHERISH the people around me.
I don't support the developer like I used to when I bought the game and I don't think others should either but this game is fire

OMORI is a game that inspired me to make a change in my life. It helped me realize that where I was wasn't where I wanted to be and accept that nothing was going to change unless I changed. It's a game that rewards those with an eye for detail, and that's all I'm willing to say out of fear of spoiling the experience.

That being said, you should be aware that by going for the PC version, you are missing out on some content. In addition, the physical console version comes with a couple of bonus goodies. I'd probably recommend going with the Switch version unless you really want to get into modding.

I don't usually write reviews for games, but this game is an exception. THE exception. It is without a doubt the single greatest, most powerful, most beautiful, most incredible game this random Internet person has ever EVER played. It is the Magnum Opus of gaming as a form of literature and I genuinely cannot recommend it enough.

The best way to describe it without spoiling it is that once you get into Omori, it subsumes you. It consumes every single thought in your brain, even long after you've finished playing it. Every person I know personally who has played this game, and every person I've witnessed play on the Internet, has been changed in some way as a direct consequence of playing the game. They look at the world differently than they used to, I look at the world differently than I used to. 

Every moment in the game is some form of hilariously funny, beautifully endearing, amazing to look at, deeply saddening, horrifyingly sorrowful, and finally, the 'ultimate catharsis', as Detective Mar proclaims in his video essay on the game. It's soundtrack is perfect, it's visuals and art are perfect, it's story is heartbreakingly brilliant, and I cannot describe how much you should play, no, *experience* this game. It is worth every penny, every minute, and every tear, laugh, smile, thought and emotion given to it. 

And remember...everything is going to be okay...

Six years late to the hype but 10/10 game, highly recommend
It is srsly one of the best games I've ever bought
every single time i replay this game, it breaks my heart. The story and development is amazing. OMORi is truly an adventure worth going through again and again.

This is one of the greatest games I've ever played. I was hesitant at first because I knew it was a slow-burner, and I knew it would take a while to pick up, but I heard too many great things about this game, and forced myself to play. I'm glad I did, because I unknowingly spent hours in the game; every new location feels completely different. The story is great, emotional, has an insane plot twist, and the gameplay is addicting. 8/10 heavily recommend

This game tragically rewards me for interacting with every inanimate object and wall i see
i love it so much.

this game is the loml. I cannot ever part with it. This is so painful to play but at the same time I love the headspace so much, I can probably spend hours in headspace. The story is also so though to digest and the characters are so well written. It always gets me emotional no matter which run I am on. The songs oh my lord, I had them playing on repeat and it was the most listened to artist 3 years ago.
One of the most impactful experiences I have ever had gaming. Wow. Absolutely incredible.

january 1st, i got this game 50% off for a 2025 new years sale. i would end up playing my first couple of hours on christmas. then, i left it in the dust for a bit, and found the energy to pick it back up the 22nd of may. i finished the game 5th of june with a friend voicing the characters with me, a four hour recording, and a disheartened feeling that the game was finally over...

i don't even know where to start! the plot twist was deeply unexpected and i find that to be one of my favorite things about the game. because with how everyone is presented in omori's dream world, you get the first impression that they're very straightforward and distinguishable. however, once you enter the real world as sunny, you begin to realize that the characters you came to know and love are actually incredibly flat and dull in the dream world. stepping into the real world allows these characters to take shape, become more rounded, and build a story that you can't help but become attached to. everyone's grown up differently, shaped by their grief and environment, but it's almost unreasonable to be angry or dislike them for it because it's all meant to be real. unfiltered. transparent! 
realizing then that sunny is an unreliable narrator, the concept of escapism vs. reality becomes incredibly nuanced as the two identities omori and sunny struggle to grasp their trauma. these identities aren't particularly separate but it's supposed to resemble sunny's fractured mind after his sister's death: omori meant to suppress the trauma in order to protect sunny and sunny meant to find the truth with the support of his friends. furthermore, sunny and basil's dynamic is a perfect example of someone whose trying to overcome their grief and someone who let it consume them entirely. 
the game is not only a beautiful depiction of how grief shapes people differently, but it also represents how these differences can be overcome and that forgiveness is an option. of course, every character has their own values they hold onto, but it goes to show that the process towards forgiveness isn't always linear. not only do other people deserve to forgive others, you deserve to forgive yourself, and this game emulates that uniquely within every character interaction.
aside from that, i absolutely love the designs of this game. i enjoyed the world exploration and journeys the friend group went on as it all held a greater meaning behind it. the discography for this game is insane, how they're able to really shape the emotion of a scene through character themes and brutal sfx's. furthermore, i just can't get over the art. all of the drawings are super raw with emotion and its lighthearted, playful artstyle makes it an all time favorite. i'm incredibly pleased with how every ending turns out, whether it's devastating or relieving, and the story is just so neatly wrapped up!
oh yeah, and i really loved aubrey. i could probably write an essay about her entire character if i wanted to but she's just someone that i connected with deeply. she's incredibly relatable in how she behaves with her friends, how she responds to grief, her chaotic environment, etc. the fact that she admired mari, one of the older figures of the group, and even made fun promises with her is how i imagine myself with some of the people i admire in my life. it essentially comes out to how losing someone close to you is also just as detrimental as losing a family member because it feels like the adjacent. 

uuum but yeah i think i like the game a little idk tho :/ i GUESS i'll recommend it or whatever.....

Enjoyed this game way more than I expected. Favorite things about the game had to be the soundtrack and story, and I'm already a fan of RPG's so I already expected the combat to be good. 9/10 Totally recommend if you're into RPG's.
A psychological j-rpg horror with a very unique and charming art style and spin to it. It's a bit of a slow burn but I highly recommend to those who haven't played.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

i really enjoyed omori but omfg the ending of that game had me heated. absolutely loved the weirdness and the aesthetics, the music was so poignant and beautiful. the RPG aspect leaves a bit to be desired (needed more bosses!) and the pacing was aaaaallllll over the place. i found it really fresh and new to start with and irritating to end with. gets very exposition heavy and can be super easy to miss story information, items, etc. which is super frustrating. still, played it for a week straight!

I first played this game when it came out and have since played it quite a few times. Hehe, on here and my switch. 

Visually, the game is nostalgic and lovable, the sketched out drawings holding so much heart in them, and the soundtrack bringing me to tears or having me jumping in my seat plenty of times. 

The battle system is really enjoyable especially the incorporations of "emotions" into fights, the only issue with the gameplay itself is that the game is Quite long and can have slow pacing at some points. For me, that wasn't a big issue because I just love to be in the world of OMORI! 

I somewhat used the game itself as escapism from, ironically, trauma and amnesia + related mental issues I was experiencing, but this game is genuinely what got me through it all. I have grown and changed from someone who was nearly the same as Sunny, to someone who takes risks and ventures further into reality everyday, and this game continues to inspire me and drive me forward to always face my fears and do right by the people I love. 

If you have been through similar things, sought out a dreamlike world to get away from your stress, and found that you can never run too far from what you are so afraid of... Play this game. I love every character, every dialogue, and every moment where you get closer to the horrifying truth that fuels this fantastical and quirky world. Take care of yourself, all it costs is your love!

This game has one of the best stories in gaming, especially if you're someone who gets attached to characters easily. That being said, I do not vibe with the gameplay at all. Maybe I just don't enjoy turn based combat, but I feel like the gameplay has next to no sauce to speak of. The pacing can be kinda off too. That being said, the story is definitely captivating enough to hold your attention, even if you don't enjoy turn based combat. Hearing the soundtrack randomly on the internet still makes me a bit emotional.

this is my favourite game ever made its pretty much perfect in every aspect from story, characters, art style and combat this fact makes me very sad there will never be a sequel but i wish all the best for this game
I love horror pixel game and this game is good for anyone who likes horror pixel game. The game has a good storyline too. I enjoyed playing this game.

Literally, the only reason I bought this was that on one fateful day I saw this game on Steam for cheap and thought, "I'm someone who hates horror, but HEY, this is pixel graphics, so it can't be that bad. I was unfortunately mistaken, as this game does know horror, and it was a pretty fun experience. What made it better was that I played this with friends, and we all voiced different characters while all experiencing this game blind (except one person). All in all, the music and art style were great, and I already love RPGs, and the fact I got to experience it with other people made it even better (cause I wasn't alone : )) 9/10
Amazing game, very rich in characters and themes. I'm unashamed to admit it made me cry a million times, wish I could play it for the first time again.
Wow. just wow, absolute masterpiece of a game. the art the story the combat literally everything connects so well. highly recommend

8 hours in and i've got to say this is a very interesting game. it takes a while to pick up and is rather disturbing. at least it is to me but perhaps i scare easily. its super creative and i am interested where the story will take me.
Great game, definitely covers very deep topics that everyone should be made aware of, executed beautifully.

this game hit me like a truck when i first finished it but i like it less and less whenever i come back. despite the overflowing sea of mid this game offers, the emotional core is so strong its somehow still worth playing despite it all

Classic RPG gameplay, beautiful art and a truly jarring yet moving story. The battles are adequately challenging and the puzzles are fun. Great characterisation, especially given that the characters are children. I'm just about ready for a replay, one of my all time favourite games.
This game has changed my life it has opened my eyes up to something ive never thought of before I do reccomend everyone at least checks this out once

Times cried: 2
Times almost cried: ~5.
This game is an intimate, one-on-one dialogue with the player, a game about tragedy and it's avoidance. I can't explain this feeling, but I want to hug this game. I would absolutely recommend this to anyone feeling broken or lonely. It's life changing. Gotta be one of my favorite games OAT.

edit: 3 days later i'm still crying like a ♥♥♥♥♥

+very good story
+fun characters
+cool artstyle
+indie game

OMORI is a RPG with satisfying turn-based combat which posseses a satisfying art style. 

The story revolves around a boy switching between the real world and his dream realm, with 3 days to reconcile with a trauma he experienced years ago. The narrative is emotional and psychotic, able to switch tones in an instant. Nonetheless, one could also easily play this game for cute vibes, as the game is full of it from time to time. 

There is an immense amount of content in the game with ton of secrets. There are multiple routes and endings, opening up enjoyment for several playthroughs in which you'll proabably find something new each time. 

The soundtrack is absolutely incredible and some tracks are absolute vibes.  

All in all, whatever the fandom might be doing, since I've heard it was infamous, I'd recommend this game if you like turn-based combat. The art design of the entire game is very creative and fun to look at. 
8,5/10
one of my favorite games great story and has a bunch of content

Omori is by far the best game i've ever played. played for the first time on Xbox 3 years ago before it was taken down and fell in love with it. I don't know where i would be without this game or the memories i've made with the people I love through it. i cant give the team enough praise (or money lol) for what they've created.

Omori balances incredible storytelling and clever game mechanics in a rather charming way. Plenty of witty jokes and silly references juxtaposed between all the trauma. Absolutely a blast, and definitely worth replaying.

What an amazing experience. These kids were like my best childhood friends by the end. The only downside was a deep yearning to make everything better for them for some of rough things they deal with, but i guess that's what makes it so touching. Very powerful. I'll be thinking about it for a long, long time.
One of the best games I have ever played. Amazing story telling, I recommend it and talk about it to my friends still and its almost 3 years since the last time I played. PLEASE PLAY ITS SO COOL!

pros:
- beautiful art style 
- soundtrack id listen to unironically
- has a depressing but excellent storyline
- relatable and loveable cast
cons:
- its hard to navigate and fight with tears in your eyes
- gave me severe bathmophobia

I know the beginning can seem really boring, and that's why I quit the game before, but such a beginning is necessary. The gameplay is just okay, typical RPG Maker battles with very unique emotions and follow-up systems, the music is pleasing to the ear. The art style is truly unique, in some moments it even seems liminal, which can evoke certain emotions, and that's sooo wonderful! The game has A LOOOT of music that will appeal to a wide range of listeners. The game is positioned as a psychological horror, but the beginning doesn't have anything special or interesting to do with that, and that's how it should be. If you don't empathize with the main characters and only see the key and moments throughout the game, those moments will lose their meaning because they are all climactic and necessary for the ending. This isn't just a game, it's art, and when a game is art, gameplay is less important, you need to feel the world, the characters, the atmosphere. Spend some time with this game, when you reach the Black Space, you will understand what I am talking about. I won't talk about the plot or the meaning of this game, you won't understand it unless you played it. It was the most emotional experience I had in the last 3 months...

Art.

incredible. life changing. story is 1 billion gazillion/10 and character development 1000/10. emotional attachment is infinity/10. before playing this game, i doubted everyone's claims saying that this game breaks you but...i see what they're talking about now. oh my goodness.

A beautiful story about the impact of grief, guilt, depression, acceptance, and all the other little things that are overlooked in this topic. Easily my favorite game of all time and impacted my life beyond words, its inspired me and I think it could inspire many others who touch this story.
awesome game, I've loved it for a couple years now and play it 3 to 7 times each month, I HIGHLY suggest playing!

This game definitely gets a bad rep for the developers not so great past, but putting that aside the game is actually very fun, it manages to incorporate the themes of guilt, isolation and depression without losing any spark in the gameplay. The mix between gameplay mechanically and narratively is what truly makes this game stand out to me and I think it was done extremely well
Absolutely beautiful game. Definitely a must-try if you love Earthbound-like RPG's, and a great story.
just got all of the achievements and what a wonderful game :') just go ahead and play it already!!! great story, art style and soundtrack especially!! easily one of my most favorite games of all time

I played this back in 2022, and it genuinely changed the way I view friendships forever. This is by far my favourite piece of media of all time. Better than any movie I've watched, game I've played, you name it. This is the best of them all. Omocat, you cooked beyond belief.
after finishing omori i now can proudly say i fell depressed. this is one of the best games ive ever played in my whole life.
colorful and funny but traumatizing
So so peak, I hadn't seen anything on this before I gave it a go, I got super hooked instantly. The characters are all so well written, the whole bloody thing is 9/10.

OMORI is genuinely moving in its exploration of guilt, repression of memory, and the need for reconciliation. There's a lot to do, a lot to explore, much well-written and engaging dialogue to have, many moments of whimsy and terror and joy and sorrow. And as a bonus hook, I'd even say this—most of that was just you trying to distract yourself. Keeping something to the back of your mind.

Funnily enough, the OMORI fandom also likes to distract themselves from crippling despair by posting the silliest stuff on the Internet. Like protagonist, like player I suppose.

My only real problem is with my utter disbelief with a certain part of the ending. You'd know once you get there, [spoiler]or in my case, once you play through the Good Ending still thinking that Mari actually did what she did and only find out The Truth through the Wikipedia page of all places,[/spoiler] but I don't think it's a fatal flaw. You can easily resolve it yourself with some imagination, just like I accidentally did.

In any case, get this game if you are interested with it in any way. It'll win you over.

"It takes a strong man to deny what's in front of him."

"Stronger than you were."

"Whatever you say, Sunny. No matter what happens next, don't be too hard on yourself.

Even now, after all you've done,

You can still go home.

Lucky you."
this game is great! it helps you how to properly socialize with your sister near a flight of stairs!
Peak game. I cried. like 4 times. I might play it again, and then cry again.
this game is so cool, great vibes and combat feels fresh. would recommend to anyone!

Kinda overrated... the combat system is interesting, but it gets repetitive. The art and music is great but the game overall could have been way shorter. The game takes some very heavy subjects (depression, suicide, etc) and just makes a show out of them by showing a bunch of weird creepypasta art. People praise how well mental illness is depicted in this, but I feel like it just romanticizes it. [spoiler] You must repeatedly stab yourself to advance in the game. [/spoiler] I think the creators of Omori were trying too hard to do something different and sacrificed their storytelling for shock value. The writers were ultimately using suicide and self-harm as a plot device, and a way to 'shock' the player, in a pretty gross way. This would have worked for me if i was a 12 year old experiencing strong emotions for the first time.

Also the plot twist legit makes no sense.
[spoiler] So Sunny accidently kills Mari and him and Basil decide to stage it as a suicide. Two kids carrying a body up the stairs, back down and then hanging it to a tree. And I'm supposed to believe everyone just forgives him after he reveals the truth three years later.
I really don't know why the game needed this plot twist that is so poorly written and hinders the game overall.  [/spoiler]
